reasoning: The Lean formalization adds `MeasureTheory.IntegrableOn u (Set.Icc 0 1)` as a hypothesis on u. While the literal informal statement says "real-valued function u" without explicit regularity, the integral equation u(x) = 1 + λ∫_x^1 u(y)u(y-x) dy only makes classical mathematical sense when u is at least integrable on [0,1] — this is a standard implicit premise of any integral equation in classical analysis. The original Putnam 1967 A4 problem explicitly assumes u is continuous on [0,1] (which implies integrability), so the informal statement already carries an implicit regularity assumption. The IntegrableOn condition is a natural, minimal regularity requirement that makes the formalization well-typed in Mathlib's integration framework (which otherwise sets integrals of non-integrable functions to 0). This fits the "implicit premise made explicit" pattern described in the guidelines, so it should be judged faithful rather than weaker.
